_meta.description: ILLUSTRATIVE SAMPLE fee schedule + capability data for a fictional payment-processing service line. Replaces the labor catalog when the Cost Writer drafts cost narratives for payment-processing RFPs. All rates and attestations here are invented. Copy to data/pricing/payment_systems.json and replace with your own data to run the system.

_usage_notes_for_agents.rate_model_caveat: Standard model here is revenue-share. When an RFP asks for interchange-plus or flat-rate, propose an alternative from volume_adjusted_offering and flag the change.
_usage_notes_for_agents.pci_level_disclosure: Always disclose the current PCI level honestly and present the roadmap; never overstate.
_usage_notes_for_agents.hardware_strategy: If in-house hardware is not maintained, surface a subcontractor/partner arrangement; partner identity is [NEEDS_HUMAN].
_usage_notes_for_agents.fee_units_explanation: Percentages are decimals (0.0300 = 3.00%); render as `3.00%` in narrative.

pricing_models_supported.description: A percentage of every successfully collected payment.
Interchange cost plus a fixed markup.

volume_adjusted_offering._description: Fallback structure for procurements that want interchange-plus/flat-rate.
volume_adjusted_offering.pricing_strategy: Build a county-tier structure off the ranges below.
volume_adjusted_offering._writer_guidance: Disclose that standard rates are volume-dependent and may be restructured.

hardware_options.partner_selection_strategy: Select per RFP volume + terminal requirements.
hardware_options._writer_guidance: Never claim in-house hardware; name partner only after [NEEDS_HUMAN] confirmation.

compliance_attestations.pci_dss_disclosure_guidance: Disclose current level honestly and present the roadmap.

our_cost_basis._purpose: Sample internal cost basis for margin math. Never disclosed in proposals.

merchant_account_capabilities._recovery_rate_caveat: Sample claim; must be substantiated before use in a proposal.
